---
title: Reusable helpers
---

A recipe is plain Python, so factor repeated setup into a function. A
`@contextmanager` that composes stage scopes drops into a `with` line next to the
built-in scopes.

## Wrap repeated mounts in a helper

This recipe defines a `cargo` helper that opens two cache mounts, then reuses it
around the build:

<!-- gd-embed: helpers.py -->

Render it with `--dev=true`:

```dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM rust:1.83-slim AS base
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cargo/registry,sharing=shared --mount=type=cache,target=/app/target,sharing=shared \
  cargo build --release \
  && cargo test
```

## How it composes

`cargo(s)` is a context manager that opens `s.cache(...)` twice and yields. In the
`with cargo(s), s.run() as r:` line it sits alongside `s.run()`, so both cache
mounts apply to every command in the block. Drop the helper into any stage that
needs the same caches.

Helpers can take more than a stage, branch on config, or wrap secrets and binds.
They are ordinary Python. Reach for `current_stage()` when a helper should act on
whichever stage is open without taking it as an argument.
